title: cladopus queenslandicus
intro: Cladopus queenslandicus, also known as the Queensland water rot, is a species of flowering plant in the family Cephalotaceae, native to Australia. It has been used traditionally for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant is said to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and supporting the immune system. In terms of herbal preparations, Cladopus queenslandicus is often used to make infusions, tinctures, and ointments, which can be applied topically or taken internally to harness its medicinal properties.
